name: Jensen Huang
executiveSummary:
Jensen Huang is a Taiwanese-American billionaire business magnate and electrical engineer. He is the co-founder, president, and CEO of Nvidia, a technology company that designs graphics processing units (GPUs) and AI hardware.

Under Huang's leadership, Nvidia has transitioned from a video game graphics company to the backbone of the global artificial intelligence boom, making it one of the most valuable companies in world history, with a market cap surpassing $3 trillion.
biography:
Huang was born in Tainan, Taiwan. His family moved to Thailand when he was a child, and he was later sent to the United States. He earned his undergraduate degree in electrical engineering from Oregon State University in 1984, and a master's degree in electrical engineering from Stanford University in 1992.

Before founding Nvidia, Huang worked at LSI Logic and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). In 1993, at the age of 30, he co-founded Nvidia with Chris Malachowsky and Curtis Priem. As CEO, Huang successfully guided the company through the volatile semiconductor market, initially focusing on GPUs for PC gaming.

Huang recognized the potential of GPUs for parallel processing early on. By developing the CUDA software platform, he enabled GPUs to be used for general-purpose computing, which directly sparked the modern deep learning and AI revolution. His signature leather jacket and charismatic keynote speeches have made him a tech icon.

recentNews.title: Blackwell Architecture Launch

recentNews.title: Nvidia Market Cap Milestones
recentNews.description: Led Nvidia to become the world's most valuable public company temporarily, surpassing Apple and Microsoft.
